datalab-a738993919a50143787084ee7ce6e2f2.description: Diagrama de agrupamento hierárquico com 5 elementos (1, 2, 3, 4, 5). À esquerda, uma seta apontando para baixo indica o processo 'Por divisão', onde o conjunto {1,2,3,4,5} se divide em {1,2} e {3,4,5}, depois {1,2} em {1} e {2}, e {3,4,5} em {3} e {4,5}, finalmente {4,5} em {4} e {5}. À direita, uma seta apontando para cima indica o processo 'aglomerativo', onde os elementos {1}, {2}, {3}, {4}, {5} se agrupam sucessivamente em {4,5}, {3,4,5}, e finalmente {1,2,3,4,5}.

Below these are two more root nodes: 'E(5,3), F(6,4)' and 'G(7,3)'. 'E(5,3), F(6,4)' branches into two child nodes: 'E(5,3)' and 'F(6,4)'. The bottom row of nodes is labeled C1 through C7. The first merge is between A and B at distance 1, forming cluster C1. The next merge is between E and F at distance 2, forming cluster C5. Subsequent merges occur at higher distances: C3 and C4 merge at distance 3; C5 and C6 merge at distance 2; C1 and C2 merge at distance 4; C3, C4, C5, and C6 merge at distance 5; finally, all clusters merge at distance 8, with G joining at the end.

Se 'Solteiro, Divorc.', a decisão vai para 'Restituição'. Se 'S', a decisão é 'N'. Se 'N', a decisão vai para 'Renda'. Se 'Renda' <= 80K, a decisão é 'N'. Se 'Renda' > 80K, a decisão é 'S'.

datalab-8307f6b04df072c9332f9987e034272c.description: Diagram illustrating the classification process. A 'Conjunto de Treino' (Training Set) is input to a 'Classificador' (Classifier), which outputs a 'Modelo' (Model). A 'Conjunto de Teste' (Test Set) is also input to the 'Modelo'.

datalab-81a4cbf0b3c4cbc065efdf8f800dadde.description: Decision tree diagram showing the classification process for the test data. The root node is 'Restituição'. A red dashed arrow points to it. The tree has two main branches: 'S' (Sim) leading to a leaf node 'N' (Não), and 'N' (Não) leading to a node 'Status Conjugal'. From 'Status Conjugal', there are two branches: 'Solteiro, Divorc.' leading to a node 'Renda', and 'Casado' leading to a leaf node 'N'. From 'Renda', there are two branches: '<= 80K' leading to a leaf node 'N', and '> 80K' leading to a leaf node 'S' (Sim).

datalab-c5655e700cc3e9aac7e9f4f07f30264d.description: Decision tree diagram and a test data table.

'Status Conjugal' (Solteiro/Divorc. leads to 'Renda', Casado leads to 'N'). 'Renda' (<= 80K leads to 'N', > 80K leads to 'S'). A red dashed arrow points from the test data row to the 'N' branch of 'Restituição'.

datalab-d734a6ea1b381280f043fcf70391b6db.description: Decision tree diagram and test data table showing the application of a model to test data.

datalab-73b5cce955ba9415a98791db7b0080ad.description: Decision tree diagram and a test data table. The tree starts with 'Restituição' (S leads to N, N leads to 'Status Conjugal'). datalab-26d664119ad25250780f554633444e54.description: Decision tree diagram for predicting 'Calote ?' based on 'Restituição', 'Status Conjugal', and 'Renda'.

datalab-d17aa1fcc3b86503ad1dd0717a6c34c2.description: Diagram illustrating the Hunt algorithm for decision tree construction. It shows three stages of tree growth: 1. Initial state: A single blue node 'N'. 2. First split: A yellow node 'Restituição' with 'S' (Yes) leading to blue node 'N' and 'N' (No) leading to blue node 'S'. 3. Further splits: The 'N' branch from 'Restituição' leads to another yellow node 'Status Conjugal', which splits into 'S' (Yes) leading to blue node 'S' and 'Casado' (Married) leading to blue node 'N'. Regression Optimal-fitting: A smooth parabolic curve that follows the general trend of the U-shaped blue dots. Regression Over-fitting: A highly erratic, jagged line that passes through every single blue dot, capturing noise rather than the trend. Classification Under-fitting: A simple straight line separating blue and red dots, misclassifying several points on both sides. Classification Optimal-fitting: A smooth curved boundary that effectively separates the cluster of red dots from the surrounding blue dots. Classification Over-fitting: A complex, convoluted boundary that twists to isolate every single red dot, including outliers, from the blue dots.

datalab-284768c5ee1a05aa1b9ce396f606a040.description: A flowchart illustrating the architecture of a conversational agent. The process starts with 'Input from User' leading to 'Speech Recognition', which outputs a 'Word Sequence' to 'NL Understanding'. 'NL Understanding' uses 'World Knowledge' and produces a 'Semantic Interpretation'. A decision diamond '?' evaluates if the interpretation is 'incomplete' or 'complete'. If 'incomplete', it goes to 'Dialog Management', which uses 'Interaction Context' and sends a 'Clarifying Question' via 'Speech Synthesis' back to the user. 'Dialog Management' also identifies 'Missing Elements'. If 'complete', it goes to 'Action Selection', which uses 'World Knowledge' and produces an 'Inferred User Intent' and 'Best Outcome'. 'Action Selection' then uses 'Speech Synthesis' to produce an 'Output to User'. A feedback loop labeled 'Elicitation' connects the output back to the initial input.

datalab-eb03559a4d92ea9ebd63ea9be663c50a.description: Diagram illustrating Precision with two Venn diagrams. The left diagram shows a yellow circle (relevant items) and a blue circle (retrieved items). The intersection is labeled 'Relevant items - retrieved'. The part of the yellow circle not in the intersection is labeled 'Relevant items - not retrieved'. The part of the blue circle not in the intersection is labeled 'Irrelevant items - retrieved'. The right diagram shows a yellow circle labeled 'A' (relevant items retrieved) and a blue circle labeled 'C' (irrelevant items retrieved). The formula for Precision is given as \frac{A}{A+C} \times 100%.

datalab-ae53f90bb87d6d09e2d6b5278d7c338f.description: Venn diagram illustrating Precision. A yellow circle labeled 'A' represents relevant records retrieved. A blue circle labeled 'C' represents irrelevant records retrieved. The intersection of the two circles is shaded with diagonal lines. The formula for Precision is given as (A / (A+C)) * 100%.

datalab-dd5771673aececa53d42ece89218299d.description: Two Venn diagrams illustrating Recall. The left diagram shows a yellow circle (relevant items) and a blue circle (retrieved items). The intersection is labeled 'Relevant items - retrieved'. The part of the yellow circle not in the intersection is labeled 'Relevant items - not retrieved'. The part of the blue circle not in the intersection is labeled 'Irrelevant items - retrieved'. The right diagram shows a yellow circle divided into two parts: a larger part labeled 'B' (Number of relevant records not retrieved) and a smaller part labeled 'A' (Number of relevant records retrieved). Below the diagram, the formula for Recall is given as RECALL: \frac{A}{A+B} \times 100%.

datalab-00504fc688ebcf131ccbeff94dfc9939.description: Venn diagram illustrating Recall. A yellow circle is divided into two parts: B (unshaded) and A (hatched).
